deep_dive.title: Autoregressive latent diffusion for 3D molecule generation
deep_dive.abstract: Three-dimensional (3D) molecule generation has been dominated by diffusion models, which achieve strong generation quality but typically require the molecular size to be specified a priori. Recent autoregressive approaches have substantially narrowed the performance gap while naturally supporting variable-length generation and conditioning on partial molecular context. However, balancing unconditional and context-conditioned generation remains challenging. We introduce KRONOS, a latent autoregressive diffusion framework that generates molecules in the latent space of a pre-trained autoencoder, jointly modeling molecular graph topology and geometry, while retaining the flexibility of autoregressive generation. We further introduce a mixed training strategy inspired by Fill-in-the Middle (FIM) paradigm, enabling both unconditional and fragment-conditioned molecular generation within a single left-to-right autoregressive model. Experiments on QM9 and GEOM-Drugs demonstrate that KRONOS achieves leading unconditional generation performance among autoregressive methods, while remaining competitive with diffusion models. Moreover, fragment-conditioned generation is achieved with negligible impact on unconditional generation performance, demonstrating that both generation paradigms can be supported within a single architecture.

deep_dive.matched_query_text: Papers central to 帮我检索自回归扩散模型的研究进展, especially work that connects or combines: autoregressive diffusion models research progress; AR-Diffusion model architecture and performance; autoregressive sequence generation with diffusion; combining autoregressive models and diffusion models; recent advances in autoregressive diffusion models; state of the art autoregressive diffusion architectures.
